example.name: basic_docs_site_structure
example.scenario: Creating a new documentation site with standard navigation hierarchy
example.user_request: "I want to create a documentation site for my React component library with getting started, guides, and API reference sections"

step.description: Define navigation hierarchy and content categories
step.approach: Analyze the component library structure and identify logical groupings. Group related components together, separate guides from API reference. Outcome: Navigation tree with 3 main categories.

step.description: Create sidebar navigation component with collapsible sections
step.approach: Implement SidebarNav with sections for Getting Started, Guides, and API Reference. Each section contains expandable child links. Add persistence for expanded state.

step.description: Build main content area with markdown rendering
step.approach: Set up markdown renderer with syntax highlighting (using Prism or Shiki). Configure typography scale for headings, body, and code. Add proper spacing and content container styles.

step.description: Implement contextual panel with TOC and related topics
step.approach: Create right-side panel that parses H2/H3 headings for table of contents. Show related topics based on frontmatter category/tags. Implement scroll-based active section highlighting.

step.description: Add top navigation with breadcrumbs and theme toggle
step.approach: Build navbar with breadcrumb trail showing current location. Add version selector dropdown. Include theme toggle with sun/moon icons.

example.completion: Created a professional documentation site structure with persistent sidebar navigation, hierarchical categories, main content area with proper typography, and contextual panel. The site is responsive and supports dark/light themes.

key_takeaways.takeaway: Start with navigation hierarchy before implementing components
Use consistent naming conventions for files and directories
Include frontmatter in all markdown files for search indexing

example.name: ai_search_integration
example.scenario: Adding AI-powered search to existing documentation site
example.user_request: "I want to add intelligent search to my docs that understands natural language queries like 'how do I configure X'"

step.description: Set up Minimax API client and search indexing
step.approach: Create search client module with Minimax API configuration. Build document indexing pipeline that processes markdown files. Index headings, code blocks, and content separately for targeted search.

Create query understanding module that parses queries like: - "how do I configure X" → intent: tutorial, topic: configuration - "show me examples of Y" → intent: examples, topic: Y - "X API reference" → intent: reference, topic: X

step.description: Build search modal with suggestions and history
step.approach: Create SearchModal component triggered by Cmd+K. Implement debounced search with suggestion dropdown. Add recent searches stored in localStorage. Include filtering options by category/content type.
Format search results to show: - Matched content with highlighted query terms - Breadcrumb path for navigation - Section/heading context - Relevance ranking indicator

example.completion: Integrated AI-powered search with natural language query understanding. Users can now search using phrases like "how do I configure authentication" and receive relevant sections, code examples, and navigation paths with proper citations.

key_takeaways.takeaway: Index content structure (headings, code) separately for targeted results
Parse query intent to improve relevance ranking
Show breadcrumb paths in results to help users understand location

example.name: responsive_mobile_sidebar
example.user_request: "The docs site works great on desktop but needs mobile-friendly navigation"

step.description: Create mobile sidebar with slide-out drawer
step.approach: Implement SidebarDrawer component that slides in from left on mobile. Use backdrop overlay to close when tapping outside. Add hamburger menu button that appears only on mobile.
Configure page transitions using CSS or animation library. Fade out current content, fade in new content with slight slide. Keep sidebar state persistent during transitions.
Ensure tap targets are at least 44px. Add swipe gesture support for opening/closing drawer. Verify pinch-zoom works correctly in code blocks.

example.completion: Added mobile-friendly sidebar with slide-out drawer, smooth page transitions, and touch-friendly interactions. The mobile experience is now on par with desktop.

key_takeaways.takeaway: Use CSS transforms for smooth drawer animation (no layout thrashing)
Test on actual mobile devices, not just responsive mode
Keep sidebar state synchronized between desktop and mobile views

example.name: syntax_highlighting_playgrounds
example.scenario: Adding syntax highlighting and interactive code playgrounds
example.user_request: "I want professional code blocks with syntax highlighting and the ability to run code examples"

step.description: Configure syntax highlighting for code blocks
step.approach: Integrate Prism.js or Shiki for syntax highlighting. Support common languages: JavaScript, TypeScript, Python, Bash, JSON. Theme should match documentation light/dark mode.
Add copy-to-clipboard button to all code blocks. Show language label in top-right corner. Support line numbers for longer examples. Add collapsible code view for very long examples.
Create sandboxed code execution environment using Web Workers or iframe. Implement live code editor with syntax highlighting. Add run, reset, and share buttons. Display output console below editor.

example.completion: Implemented professional syntax highlighting with theme support and interactive code playgrounds. Users can now run code examples directly in the documentation.

key_takeaways.takeaway: Use Web Workers or sandboxed iframes for secure code execution
Keep playground isolated from main page context
Provide clear error messages when code fails to execute
